titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.17

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=670 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:43:39.146309+00 | translation moa=671 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:43:41.799659+00

div.p:
. W . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Epsicopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . prepositis . ministris .
et Omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius terre sue . Clericis et Laicis  Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri .
nos concessisse . et Hac Carta nostra confirmasse . amicabilem pacem factam inter conuentum
et Ecclesiam de Kelch’ . ex una parte . et Comuentum et Ecclesiam de Melros ex altera parte .
super diuisis terrarum de Bowlden’ . et de Eldun . et de Dernewic’ . Quare uolumus et precipimus
quatinus predicta pax inter Conuentus et predictas domos facta . firmiter et inuiolabiliter futuris temporibus in
perpetuum teneatur . sicut in Cyrographo inter Conuentus et predictas domos inde facto euidentius
continetur . Concedimus etiam et Hac Carta nostra confirmamus amicabilem pacem factam inter
predictos Conuentus et predictas domos super controuersia que uertebatur inter predictos Conuentus et domos
prenominatas . de terra de Molle et de Cliftun’ . sicut in predicto Cyrographo inter eos facto
continetur . Testibus . Alexandro . filio . domini Regis . Philippo . de Valoniis . Camerario . Roberto . de londoniis . filio . domini Regis .
Willelmo . Cumin . DauiD . de lindeseia . Iusticiis . Oliuero . Capellano domini Regis . Willelmo . de Boscho . Clerico domini Regis .
Philippo . de Mubray . Waltero . Olifard’ . Henrico . filio . Comitis . DauiD . Philippo . de Lundin’ . DauiD . Marescallo .
Ricardo . filio . Hugonis . Herberto . de Camera . Iohanne . de Stiruelin . Ricardo . Adam . Hasteng’ . Apud Striuelin .
xxii . die Iunii .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know us to have conceded, and by this our charter to have made firm, the amicable peace made between the convent and church of Kelso on the one side, and the convent and church of Melrose on the other side, over the boundaries of the lands of Bowden, Eildon, and Darnick. Wherefore we will and instruct that the aforesaid peace made between the aforesaid convents and houses be firmly and inviolably held in times to come and for ever, just as it is more plainly specified in the cyrograph made between the aforesaid convents and houses. We have also conceded and by this our charter made firm the amicable peace made between the aforesaid convents and aforesaid houses over the dispute that was at issue between the aforesaid convents and houses aforenamed, about the land of Mow and of Clifton, just as it is specified in the aforesaid cyrograph made between the two. As witnesses: Alexander the lord king's son; Philip de Valognes, chamberlain; Robert of London, the lord king's son; William Comyn, David of Lindsay, justices; Oliver the lord king's chaplain; William del Bois, the lord king's clerk; Philip of Mowbray; Walter Oliphant; Henry the son of Earl David; Philip of Lundin; David the marischal; Richard son of Hugh; Herbert de Camera; John of Stirling; Richard, Adam Hastings. At Stirling on the 22nd day of June.
